name: Strongyloidiasis Threadworm Infection
description: A parasitic roundworm infection transmitted through skin contact with contaminated soil. The worm is unique in its ability to replicate inside the human host (auto-infection), leading to persistent chronic disease and life-threatening hyperinfection.

climateLinks.impact: Larvae survive and multiply in warm, damp, organic soil. Flooding events and inadequate sanitation spread larvae to populated farm zones.

prevention: Wearing shoes consistently in agricultural zones
triageAdvisory: Clinical features: Recurrent rash (larva currens), epigastric pain, and cough. Perform stool agar plate culture or ELISA. Treat immediately with single-dose Ivermectin or prolonged Thiabendazole to avoid fatal hyperinfection.
